methods.description: Survey of vegetation surrounding moss-polster sites up to 1,500 m, following a standard protocol to assess plant cover and community composition in different zones.

steps.description: Zone A (0−10 m): plant cover was estimated within 21 × 1 m² quadrats, one quadrat was located at the center of the sampling site, and 20 other quadrats were placed at specific distances of N, E, S, W, NE, SE, SW, and NW directions.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate plant cover in quadrats around sampling site

steps.description: Zone B (10−100 m): vegetation communities, defined based on dominant taxa, were mapped using a compass and a handheld GPS out to 100 m, and vegetation was surveyed in each community within several randomly distributed 1-m² quadrats for the open community, 6-m radius point surveys and 1-m² quadrats for semiopen and forest community.
steps.searchable_summary: map and survey vegetation communities using GPS

steps.description: Zone C (100 m-1,500 m): Google Earth image within 1,500 m from the center of each moss sample was used to identify land-cover categories, where both winter and summer images were adopted to separate deciduous from evergreen trees; plant composition for each land-cover category was obtained from the field surveys.
steps.searchable_summary: use Google Earth to identify land-cover categories

methods.method_name: RPP Estimate Validation using REVEALS Model
methods.description: Validation of relative pollen productivity (RPP) estimates using the REVEALS model with pollen counts and vegetation data from moss polsters and regional vegetation.

steps.description: Run the ERV model with pollen counts and vegetation data within 1.5 km of the 19 sites located in the southwest and the 17 sites located in the northeast of the study region to generate a new set of RPP estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: run ERV model to generate RPP estimates

steps.description: Compare the standard deviations (SDs) for all RPP estimates with previous data.
steps.searchable_summary: compare RPP estimates' SDs with previous data

methods.description: Assessment of the spatial representation of REVEALS-based vegetation abundance, comparing RVs with observed vegetation at various distances.

steps.description: Compare RVs based on pollen records from small-to middle-sized lakes with vegetation collected around 200 km.
steps.searchable_summary: compare RVs with vegetation data at 200 km

steps.description: Evaluate the similarity between RVs and OBVs collected from different distances for various taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate RVs and OBVs similarity for taxa
